svg.text: JAPANESE + ZHUANG — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 18D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— I. MATSUO BASHŌ — HAIKU (1686 CE) —
古池や 蛙飛び込む 江の音
furuike ya / kawazu tobikomu / mizu no oto
old pond / frog leaps in / sound of water
"An old pond — a frog leaps in — the sound of water." — BashŌ. Most famous haiku in history.
— II. JAPANESE PROVERB — ON MEMORY —
记憶は未来を作る。
Kioku wa mirai wo tsukuru.
"Memory creates the future." (Japanese proverb on the relationship between past and future.)
— III. JAPANESE BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
これは、未来を心に刺していた人たちによって、
あなたのために、無償で、作られました。
This / future-ACC heart-in pierced-PAST people-by / you-for / free-of-charge / was-made.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who held the future in their heart." (Japanese)
— JAPANESE NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
未来 (mirai) = future (not-yet-come). 記憶 (kioku) = memory. Kanji carry the conceptual weight.
心に刺す = to pierce the heart / to hold in heart. SOV verb-final construction preserved throughout.
SECONDARY — ZHUANG (CUENGH) — RUNNING TEXT
— I. ZHUANG PROVERB — ON MOUNTAIN AND MEMORY —
Bya sang naengh bwnroengz, vunz laeg naengh sim.
Mountain tall remember-in roots person old remember heart.
"The tall mountain holds its roots; the old person holds memory in the heart." (Zhuang proverb)
— II. ZHUANG PROVERB — ON THE RIVER (LAI YA IDIOM) —
Raemxdah rox roen, vunz rox boux.
River knows path person knows person.
"The river knows its way; a person knows another person." (Zhuang: empathy and recognition)
— III. ZHUANG BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Neix guh hawj mwngz, byaij mbouj gwn cienz,
youq vunz naengh laeng daeuj guh.
This made for you walk not eat money / by people remember forward come made.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future." (Zhuang)
— ZHUANG NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Laeng = forward/ahead — and by extension: future. Daeuj = come (directional toward speaker).
Naengh = to remember, to keep/hold in mind (lit. to sit with / to keep alongside).
Byaij mbouj gwn cienz = walk not eat money = “freely” (walk without spending money = do for free).
— LANGUAGE BRIDGE — ACROSS THE EAST CHINA SEA —
Japanese: ~125M speakers. Island language, 3-script system, pitch accent, mora-timing.
Zhuang: ~17M speakers. Largest minority language of China. SVO isolating, 6 tones, Tai-Kadai family.
Expanded radius (1500mi) bridges two languages that share East Asian cultural context via China.
PLATE 18D ALT · Japanese / Zhuang · RUNNING TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
